INTRODUCTION
This bulletin is a handbook for city officials and members of the planning
commission :
It contains the Kentucky statutes
which apply to planning and zoning .
This publication is meant primarily for cities of the third , fourth , fifth and
sixth classes ; therefore sections of the chapter on zoning and planning ( Chapter
100 ) applying to first and second class cities have been deleted . Each of the other
sections , on Regional Planning , Slum Clearance and Redevelopment , and Airport
Zoning , has been presented in its entirety . The third to sixth class grouping
includes all cities having a population of less than 20,000 .
The legislation providing authority for slum clearance and redevelopment has
been included because slum clearance and redevelopment is an integral part of
city planning . The statutes require cooperative effort between the redevelopment
agency and the city planning commission .

KENTUCKY REVISED STATUTES
CHAPTER 100
ZONING AND PLANNING
The following sections of chapter 100 are ap-
plicable to cities of the third , fourth , fifth
and sixth classes . Subheadings in brackets
have been added for easy reference .
100.010 DEFINITIONS . As used in KRS 100.320 to 100.830 , unless the context
requires otherwise :
( 1 ) " Mayor " means the chief executive of the city whether the
official designation of his office is mayor , city manager , or otherwise .
( 2 ) " Legislative body " means the chief legislative body of the city ,
whether it is a board of aldermen , general council , board of commis-
sioners , or otherwise .
( 3 ) " Streets " includes streets , avenues , boulevards , roads, lanes ,
alleys , viaducts and other ways .
(4 ) " Subdivision " means the division of a tract of land into two or
more divisions for the purpose , whether immediate or future , of sale or
of building development , and includes resubdivision .
( 5 ) " County commissioners " means the chief administrative or
legislative body of the county .
(6) " Municipal area " means the surrounding territory which bears
relation to the planning and zoning of the city .
( 7 ) " Commission ” means planning and zoning commission . ( 1942 ,
c . 176, 17 )
100.020 CONSTRUCTION OF CHAPTER . ( 1) If regulations made under author-
ity of KRS 100.320 to 100.830 require a greater width or size of yards ,
courts , or other open spaces , or require a lower height of building or less
number of stories , or require a greater percentage of lot to be left unoccu-
pied or impose other higher standards than those required in any other
statute or local ordinance or regulation , the provisions of the regulations
made under authority of KRS 100.320 to 100.830 shall govern .
( 2 ) If any other statute or local ordinance or regulation requires a
greater width or size of yards , courts , or other open space , or requires a
lower height of building or a less number of stories , or requires a greater
percentage of a lot to be left unoccupied , or imposes other higher stand-
ards than those required by the regulations made under authority of KRS
100.320 to 100.830 , the provisions of that statute or local ordinance or regu-
lation shall govern .
(3 ) ( a ) KRS 100.320 to 100.490 apply to cities of the second class .
( b ) KRS 100.500 to 100.830 apply to cities of the third , fourth , fifth
and sixth classes .

[ZONING ]
100.500 POWER TO REGULATE . The legislative body of any city of the third,
fourth , fifth and sixth class may regulate and restrict the height , number
of stories , and size of buildings and other structures , the percentage of lot
that may be occupied , the size of yards , courts and other open spaces , the
density of population , and the location and use of buildings , structures ,
and land for trade , industry , residence , or other purposes .
Cities in Jefferson County may accept benefits of Louisville and Jefferson County zoning
and planning law, KRS 100.033
100.510 DISTRICTS . The legislative body may divide the city into districts of
such number , shape , and area as it deems best suited to carry out the
purposes of KRS 100.500 to 100.600 . Within each district it may regulate
and restrict the construction , alteration , repair , or use of buildings , struc-
tures , or land . Regulations shall be uniform for each class or kind of
building in each district , but the regulations in one district may differ
from those in other districts .
100.520 PURPOSES OF REGULATIONS . Regulations shall be made in accord-
ance with a comprehensive plan and shall be designed to lessen congestion
in the streets ; to secure safety from fire , panic , and other dangers ; to
promote health and the general welfare ; to provide adequate light and
air ; to prevent the over - crowding of land ; to avoid undue concentration
of population ; to facilitate the adequate provision of transportation , water,
sewerage , schools , parks , and other public requirements . The regulations
shall be made with reasonable consideration of the character of the dis-
trict and its peculiar suitability for particular uses , and with a view to
conserving the value of buildings and encouraging the most appropriate
use of land .
100.530 ESTABLISHMENT OF REGULATIONS ; NOTICE ; HEARING . The legis-
lative body shall provide for the manner in which regulations and restric-
tions and the boundaries of districts shall be established , enforced , and
amended . No regulation , restriction , or boundary shall become effec-
tive until after a public hearing , at which parties in interest and citizens
shall have an opportunity to be heard . At least fifteen days before the
hearing one notice shall be published in an official newspaper , or paper of
general circulation in the city , stating the time and place of the hearing .
100.540 CHANGES IN REGULATIONS . All regulations , restrictions , and bound-
aries may from time to time be amended or repealed . In case of a protest
against the change , signed by the owners of thirty - five percent or more
either of the area of the lots included in the proposed change , or of those
immediately adjacent in the rear thereof extending 150 feet therefrom ,
or of those directly opposite thereto extending 150 feet from the street
frontage of the opposite lots , an amendment shall become effective only
by the favorable vote of three - fourths of all the members of the legislative
body . KRS 100.530 shall apply to amendments .

100.560 BOARD OF ADJUSTMENT . ( 1) The legislative body may provide for
the appointment of a board of adjustment , and may provide that the
board of adjustment may make special exceptions to the terms of the
ordinance in harmony with its general purpose and intent and in accord-
ance with general or specific rules contained in the ordinance .
( 2) The board of adjustment shall consist of five members, each
to be appointed for a term of three years and removable for cause by
the appointing authority upon written charges and after public hearing .
Vacancies shall be filled by the legislative body for the unexpired term .
100.570 MEETINGS ; APPEALS ; NOTICE . ( 1) The board of adjustment shall
adopt rules in accordance with the provisions of ordinances adopted under
KRS 100.500 to 100.600 . Meetings of the board shall be held at the call of
the chairman and at such other times as the board determines . The
chairman , or in his absence the acting chairman , may administer oaths
and compel the attendance of witnesses . All meetings of the board shall
be open to the public . The board shall keep minutes of its proceedings ,
showing the vote of each member upon each question , or , if absent , or
failing to vote , indicating such fact , and shall keep records of its examina-
tions and other official actions . The minutes and records shall be imme-
diately filed in the office of the board and shall be a public record .

100.580 POWERS OF BOARD OF ADJUSTMENT . ( 1) The board of adjustment
may :
( a) Hear and decide appeals where it is alleged there is error in any
order or decision made by an administrative official in the enforcement
of KRS 100.500 to 100.600 or of any ordinance adopted pursuant thereto ;
(b ) Hear and decide special exceptions to the terms of the ordinance
upon which the board is required to pass under the ordinance ; and
( c) Authorize , upon appeal in specific cases , such variance from
the terms of the ordinance as will not be contrary to the public interest ,
where a literal enforcement of the provisions of the ordinance will result
in unnecessary hardship , and so that the spirit of the ordinance shall
be observed and substantial justice done .
( 2 ) In exercising its powers the board may reverse , affirm or modify
the order or decision appealed from and mav make such order or decision
as ought to be made , and to that end shall have all the powers of the
officer from whom the appeal is taken .
( 3 ) The concurring vote of four members of the board shall be
necessary to reverse , affirm , or modify any order or decision of an admin-
istrative official .
100.590 APPEAL TO CIRCUIT COURT . ( 1 ) Anv person aggrieved by any
decision of the board of adjustment , or any taxpayer , or any city officer ,
or department , board , or bureau may present to the circuit court a verified
petition stating that the decision of the board is illegal , in whole or in
part , and specifying the grounds of the illegality . Such petition shall be
presented to the court within thirty days after the filing of the decision
in the office of the board .
(2) Upon the presentation of the petition the court may allow a writ
of certiorari , directed to the board of adjustment , to review the decision
of the board . If a writ of certiorari is allowed , the court shall prescribe
therein the time within which a return must be made and served upon
the relator's attorney , which shall be not less than ten days and may be
extended by the court . The allowance of the writ shall not stay proceed-
ings upon the decision appealed from , but the court may , on application ,
on notice to the board and due cause shown , grant a restraining order .
( 3 ) The board shall not be required to return the original papers
acted upon by it, but may return certified or sworn copies or portions
thereof called for by the writ . The return shall concisely set forth such
other facts as may be pertinent to show the grounds of the decision ap-
pealed from , and shall be verified .

make and adopt a master plan for the physical development of the city
and the municipal area . The plan , with accompanying descriptive matter ,
shall show the commission's recommendations for the development of
the territory , including the general location , character and extent of
streets , subways , bridges , waterways , water fronts , playgrounds , squares ,
parks , aviation fields , and other public ways , grounds and open spaces ,
the general location of public buildings and other public property , and
the general location and extent of public utilities and terminals , whether
publicly or privately owned or operated , for water , light , sanitation ,
transportation , communication , power , and other purposes ; also the
removal , relocation , widening , narrowing , vacating , abandonment , change

utility or terminal . The master plan shall also include a zoning plan for
the control of the height , area , bulk , location , and use of buildings and
premises . As the work of making the whole master plan progresses , the
commission may from time to time adopt and publish parts of it , any
such part to cover one or more major divisions of the city or one or more
of the functional matters to be included in the plan . The commission
may from time to time amend or extend the plan .

100.720 SUBDIVISION JURISDICTION . The territorial jurisdiction of planning
commission over the subdivision of land shall include all land located in
the city and all land lying within five miles of the corporate limits of the
city and not located in any other city . If any land lying outside the cor-
porate limits of any city , but within five miles of more than one city
having a planning commission , the jurisdiction of each planning commis-
sion shall terminate at a line equidistant from the respective corporate
limits of the cities .
100.730 RECORDING AND TRANSFER OF LAND . ( 1) When a planning com-
mission has adopted a major street plan of the territory within its sub-
division jurisdiction and has filed a certified copy of the plan in the office
of the county clerk of the county in which such territory or part is lo-
cated , then no plat of a subdivision of land within such territory shall be
recorded by the county clerk until it has been approved by the planning
commission and the approval entered in writing on the plat by the chair-
man or secretary of the commission .
( 2 ) No person shall transfer or negotiate to transfer any land
located within a subdivision by reference to or by other use of a plat of a
subdivision , before such plat has been approved by the planning com-
mission and recorded in the county clerk's office . The description of such
land by metes and bounds shall not exempt the transaction from the re-
quirements of this subsection .

Before exercising the powers referred to in KRS 100.730 the planning

rangement of streets , for adequate and convenient open spaces for traffic ,
utilities , access of fire - fighting apparatus , recreation , light and air , and
for the avoidance of congestion of population , including minimum width
and area of lots .

which streets shall be graded and improved and to which water and sewer

100.750 COMMISSION'S ACTION ON THE PLAT ; EFFECT OF APPROVAL . If
the planning commission fails to approve or disapprove the plat within
thirty days after the submission of the plat to it , the plat shall be deemed
to have been approved , and a certificate to that effect shall be issued by
the commission on demand, unless the applicant for the commission's
approval waives this requirement and consents to an extension of such
period . The ground of disapproval of any plat shall be stated upon the
records of the commission . Each plat submitted to the commission shall
contain the name and address of a person to whom notice of a hearing
shall be sent , and no plat shall be acted on by the commission without
affording a hearing thereon . Notice of the time and place of the hearing
shall be sent to the said address by registered mail not less than five days
before the date fixed for the hearing . Similar notice shall be mailed to the
owners of land immediately adjoining the platted land , as their names
appear upon the plats in the county clerk's office and their addresses
appear in the directory of the city or on the tax records of the city or
county . Each plat approved by the commission shall be deemed a part
of the city plan . Approval of a plat shall not constitute an acceptance by
the public of any street or open space shown on the plat . The planning
commission may , from time to time , recommend to the legislative body
amendments of the zoning ordinace or map to conform to the commission's
recommendations for the zoning regulation of the territory in approved
subdivisions . The commission may agree with the applicant on the use ,
height , area or bulk requirements or restrictions governing buildings and
premises within the subdivision , if the requirements or restrictions do not
authorize the violation of the zoning ordinance of the city in effect at the
time . The requirements or restrictions shall be stated on the plat prior
to the approval and recording of the plat and shall have the same force
of law and be enforceable in the same manner and with the same penal-
ties , may be amended or repealed in the same manner , as a part of the
zoning ordinance or map of the city .

100.760 IMPROVEMENTS IN UNAPPROVED STREET ; ACCEPTANCE OF UN-
APPROVED STREET . ( 1) The city shall not accept , lay out , improve
or light any street , or lay or authorize water mains or sewers or connec-
tions to be laid in any street within the territory for which the planning
commission has adopted a major street plan , unless the street has been
accepted or opened or otherwise has received the legal status of a public
street prior to the adoption of the plan ; or unless the street corresponds
with a street shown on the official master plan or with a street on a
subdivision plat approved by the planning commission or with a street
on a street plat made and adopted by the commission .
( 2 ) But the legislative body may accept a street not shown on or
not corresponding with a street on the official master plan or on an ap-
proved subdivision plat or an approved street plat , if the ordinance
accepting the street is first submitted to the planning commission for its
approval and , if approved by the commission , is passed by not less than
a majority of the entire membership of the legislative body , or , if dis-
approved by the commission , is enacted or passed by not less than two-
thirds of the entire membership of the legislative body . A street ap-
proved by the planning commission upon submission by council , or a
street accepted by a two- thirds vote after disapproval by the planning
commission , shall have the status of an approved street as fully as though
it had been originally shown on the official master plan or on a subdi-
vision plat approved by the commission or had been originally platted by
the commission .
100.770 BUILDING PERMITS . After the planning commission has adopted a
major street plan of the territory within its subdivision jurisdiction no
building shall be erected nor a building permit issued within such terri-
tory unless the street giving access to the lot upon which the building is
proposed to be placed has been accepted or opened or has otherwise re-
ceived the legal status of a public street prior to that time , or unless the
street corresponds with a street shown on the official master plan or with
a street on a subdivision plat approved by the planning commission or
with a street on a street plat made and adopted by the commission or with
a street accepted by the legislative body , after submission to the planning
commission , by the favorable vote required by KRS 100.760 . Any building
erected in violation of this section shall be deemed an unlawful structure
and the building inspector or other appropriate official may cause it to
be vacated and have it removed .
100.780 STATUS OF EXISTING STATUTES . After a planning commission
acquires control over subdivisions as provided in KRS 100.730 , the juris-
diction of the planning commission over plats shall be exclusive within
the territory under its jurisdiction , and all statutory control over plats
or subdivisions of land granted by other statutes shall , in so far as in
harmony with the provisions of KRS 100.610 to 100.830 , be transferred to
the planning commission .

[ STREET RESERVATIONS ]
100.790 RESERVATION OF LOCATIONS . ( 1) After it has adopted a major
street plan of the territory within its subdivision jurisdiction or of any
major section of such territory , the planning commission may make
surveys for the exact location of the lines of streets in any portion of
such territory and may make a plat of the area surveyed , showing the
land which it recommends be reserved for future acquisition for public
streets . The commission , before adopting any such plat , shall hold a
public hearing , and give notice of the time and place of the hearing , with
a general description of the area covered by the plat , not less than ten
days previous to the time fixed therefor by one publication in a newspaper
of general circulation in the city if the area is within the city , or of general
circulation in the county if the area is outside the city . After the hearing
the commission may transmit the plat , as originally made or modified to
the legislative body , together with the commission's estimate of the time
within which the lands shown on the plat as street locations should be
acquired by the city . The legislative body may approve and adopt the
plat by resolution , or may reject or modify it with the approval of the
planning commission , or , in the event of the planning commission's dis-
approval , the legislative body may , by a favorable vote of not less than
two - thirds of its entire membership , modify the plat and adopt the modi-
fied plat . In the resolution of adoption the legislative body shall fix the
period of time for which the street locations shown upon the plat shall be
reserved for future acquisition for public use . Upon adoption the clerk
of the legislative body shall transmit one attested copy of the plat to the
county clerk of each county in which the platted land is located and retain
one copy for the purpose of public examination and hearings of claims
for compensation .
( 2 ) Approval and adoption of a plat shall not be deemed the open-
ing or establishment of any street , nor the taking of any land for street
purposes , nor for public use , nor as a public improvement , but solely as
a reservation of the street locations shown thereon , for the period speci-
fied in the resolution , for future taking or acquisition for public use .
The commission may , at any time , negotiate with the land owners for
releases of claims for damages or compensation for such reservations or
agreements indemnifying the city from such claims by others , and such
agreements shall be binding upon the owners executing them and their
successors in title . At any time after the filing of a plat with the county
clerk , and during the period specified for the reservation , the planning
commission and the owner of any land containing a reserved street
location may agree upon a modification of the location of the lines of the
proposed street . The agreement shall include a release by the owner of
any claim for compensation or damages by reason of the modification .
The commission may then make a plat corresponding to the modification
and transmit it to the legislative body , and if the modified plat is ap-
proved by the legislative body , the clerk of the legislative body shall
transmit an attested copy of the plat to the county clerk and the modified
plat shall take the place of the original plat . At any time the legislative
body may , by resolution , abandon any reservation and shall certify any
such abandonment to the county clerk .

100.800 APPRAISERS ; COMPENSATION FOR RESERVATIONS . ( 1) In the
resolution of adoption of a plat the legislative body shall appoint a board
of three appraisers and shall fix the time and place of hearings by such
board upon the amount of compensation to be paid for the reservations .
Thereupon the clerk of the legislative body shall publish in at least one
newspaper of general circulation in the city once a week for four con-
secutive weeks a notice containing a general description of the land
reserved , as shown on the plat , the provisions of the resolution including
the period of time for which such reservations are made , the time within
which claims for compensation may be filed , which shall be not less than
three nor more than six months from the date of the notice , and the time
and place of hearing shall not be set earlier than thirty days after the
date of the first publications . Such notice shall also be posted in at least
three public places in the neighborhood of the reservation .
( 2 ) The board of appraisers shall fix the amount of compensation
to be paid respectively to the owners of lands reserved for the period
of time as shown on the plat and in the resolution adopted by the legis-
lative body . Whenever the clerk of the legislative body receives , within
the specified period , any claim for compensation , he shall transmit it to
the board of appraisers . At the time and place fixed for the hearing the
board of appraisers shall hear and consider all claims presented to it in
writing or in person , including all evidence which may be presented by
claimants or other persons . The board of appraisers shall have the right
on its own initiative to investigate evidence relevant to the question of
compensation . In case of the abandonment of a reservation prior to the
time fixed for payment of compensation , the city shall be liable to the
owner of the land included within the abandoned reservation for the
expenses incurred by the owner by reason of reservation .
100.810 REPORT OF APPRAISERS ; HEARING ; ACTION OF LEGISLATIVE
BODY . ( 1 ) Within ninety days after the time fixed for the filing of
claims , the board of appraisers shall file its tentative report with the
clerk of the legislative body , setting forth its findings as to the amount
of compensation to be paid the respective owners of the lands included
within the lines of reservations as located on the approved plat . There-
upon the clerk of the legislative body shall publish once a week for two
consecutive weeks in at least one newspaper of general circulation in the
city the fact of the filing of the report of the appraisers and specify a
period of thirty days after the date of the first publication within which
objections to the report may be filed with the clerk of the legislative
body.
( 2 ) If objections are filed within this period , the clerk shall cause
the board of appraisers to hold a meeting , at which the objections shall
be transmitted to the board , and the board may modify its report . The
report in its original or modified form shall be transmitted to the legis-
lative body by its clerk . Before passing on the report , the legislative
body may return it to the board of appraisers for reconsideration , and
the board may upon further consideration transmit its former or a modi-
fied report to the legislative body for approval .

REGIONAL PLANNING
147.130 CREATION AND MEMBERSHIP OF REGIONAL PLANNING COM-
MISSION . ( 1) The planning commission of any city of the third , fourth ,
fifth or sixth class , the fiscal court of any county or any one hundred
citizens , by signed petition , may apply to the Governor for the establish-
ment of a region for planning purposes and the appointment of a regional
planning commission for that region . The Governor shall hold at least
one public hearing upon the application , and shall officially proclaim the
time and place of the hearing . If the Governor finds that , by reason of
urban development not corresponding to existing municipal boundary
lines or by reason of other trends in the growth and distribution of popu-
lation , commerce and industry , or by reason of topographic or other
conditions , two or more separate cities of the third , fourth , fifth or sixth
class or the territory of one or more such cities and neighboring non-
municipal territory have common problems of such nature as to be in-
capable of intelligent , economical and adequate solution by means of the
separate planning of each separate political unit , and require , for solution ,
a general plan of the physical development of the entire area of such
cities or territories as a whole , he shall grant the application , define the
boundaries of the region and appoint a regional planning commission .
(2) The commission shall consist of nine persons . Of the nine first
appointed , one shall serve for one year , two for two years , one for three
years , two for four years , and one for five years . The other two and all
subsequent appointees shall serve for terms of six years . If at the time
of his appointment an appointee is a public officer or in the public service
of the state or any of its political subdivisions , and his incumbency as
such expires previous to the term for which he is appointed on the
regional planning commission , his term on the commission shall terminate
with the expiration of his incumbency as such public officer or servant
unless it is extended by the Governor , in which case the extension shall
be for the remainder of the term for which he was originally appointed .
The members shall serve without compensation but shall be paid neces-
sary expenses incurred in the performance of their duties . They may ,
after a public hearing , be removed by the Governor for inefficiency ,
neglect of duty or malfeasance in office , and he shall file a written
statement of his reasons for the removal .

147.150 POWERS AND DUTIES OF THE COMMISSION . Any regional planning
commission may make , adopt , amend and extend a master regional plan

comprehensive studies of the present and future development of the
region , with due regard to its relation to neighboring regions and the
state as a whole , and to neighboring states . The plan , including maps,
charts , diagrams and descriptive matter shall show the commission's
recommendations for the physical development of the region and may
include among other things the general location , extent and character
of streets , avenues , boulevards , roads , lanes , alleys , viaducts , parks and
other public ways , grounds and open spaces , public properties and public
utilities that affect the development of the region as a whole or more
than one political subdivision of the state within the region . It may also
include the general location of forests , agricultural and open development
areas for purposes of conservation , food and water supply , sanitary and
drainage facilities , or the protection of future urban development ; and
a zoning plan for the control of the height and area , or bulk , location and
use of buildings and premises , and of the density of population . The
master plan shall be made with the general purpose of guiding and
accomplishing a coordinated , adjusted and harmonious development of
the region and of public improvements and utilities that do not begin
and terminate within the boundaries , or do not relate exclusively to the
development , of any single city of the third , fourth , fifth or sixth class ,
and which will, in accordance with the present and future needs of the
region and the state , best promote the general welfare and be most
efficient and economical .
147.160 CERTIFICATION OF REGIONAL PLAN . The regional planning com-
mission , after adopting the regional plan , shall certify copies to the
Governor , to the planning commission of each city of the third , fourth ,

each county wholly or partly included in the region , and to other organ-
ized taxing districts or political subdivisions wholly or partly included
in the region .

regional planning commission . Adoption shall be in accordance with the
procedure specified in Chapter 100 for the adoption of plans by municipal
planning commissions of such cities . When adopted , it shall have the
force and effect within the city as is provided by Chapter 100 for plans

such modification in writing , the development plan shall be changed ac-
cordingly . No modification of a plan shall affect , without the written
consent of the owner or lessee , as the case may be , any property pre-
viously sold, leased , or exchanged by the agency . ( 1950 , с. 119 , § 15;

( 2) " Airport hazard " means any structure , object of natural growth ,

court .

including , but without limitation , buildings , towers , smoke - stacks , and
overhead transmission lines .

fiscal court . ( 1946 , c . 49,1 )
